instructions.name: Maestro Issue Triage
instructions.objective: Detect issues across the project, triage them, assign agents, and track resolution — with CEO escalation for major findings

llm.mandate: Security issues are ALWAYS triaged first regardless of other priorities
Every issue gets an owner (agent) and a clear action
Major issues (architecture changes, new efforts) trigger CEO approval gate

step.goal: Gather all signals of issues from across the project
step.action: Display: "⚔️ ISSUE TRIAGE — Scanning for problems..."
CHECK SOURCE 1 — Agent Communications: Read all files in team/_memory/_comms/findings/ Read all files in team/_memory/_comms/requests/ Identify unprocessed items (no processed: true in frontmatter)

CHECK SOURCE 2 — Sprint State: Read sprint-status.yaml Flag: stories stuck in-progress for multiple sessions Flag: stories in review for too long Flag: stories with no story file Flag: completed stories that skipped code review Flag: completed epics without retrospectives

CHECK SOURCE 3 — Codebase Health: If project-context.md exists, read it for known issues Run context generation freshness check Check for TODO/FIXME/HACK comments in source code (if src/ exists) Check for failing tests (if test runner is configured) Check for linting errors (if linter is configured)

CHECK SOURCE 4 — Security Scan: Reference team/engine/security-gate.xml universal checklist Scan for hardcoded secrets (strings matching API key patterns, passwords in config) Check for dependencies with known vulnerabilities (if package manager exists) Check for files that should be gitignored but aren't (.env, credentials, keys)

CHECK SOURCE 5 — Documentation Gaps: Check if README exists and is up to date Check if API docs exist for all endpoints (if api-index.md exists) Check if architecture docs match actual codebase structure
CHECK SOURCE 6 — Agent Memory Staleness: Check team/_memory/ for agent mission files Flag missions that reference completed or changed work Flag agents with no mission who should have one

step.goal: Classify each issue and assign priority
step.action:
For each issue found, classify:
TYPE: - 🔴 SECURITY — Vulnerability, exposure, compliance gap - 🟠 BUG — Something broken that was previously working - 🟡 DESIGN — Missing or incomplete design/architecture - 🔵 BUILD — Feature or capability that needs to be created - ⚪ DEBT — Technical debt, refactoring, cleanup - 📄 DOCS — Missing or stale documentation
PRIORITY (using these criteria): - CRITICAL: Security vulnerabilities, data loss risks, blocking all progress - HIGH: Broken functionality, blocking specific stories, compliance gaps - MEDIUM: Design gaps, missing tests, stale documentation - LOW: Tech debt, minor cleanup, nice-to-have improvements
CEO ESCALATION: - If fixing this requires architecture changes → CEO approval needed - If fixing this requires new effort (not in current sprint) → CEO approval needed - If this is a security incident → CEO must be informed - Otherwise → agents can handle autonomously

step.goal: Assign each issue to the right agent with clear instructions
step.action:
For each issue, determine the best agent:
ASSIGNMENT RULES: - 🔴 SECURITY → Shield (Security Auditor) as primary, Dev as secondary for fixes - 🟠 BUG → Dev (Amelia) as primary, Tea (Murat) for test coverage - 🟡 DESIGN → Architect (Winston) or UX Designer (Sally) depending on domain - 🔵 BUILD → Dev (Amelia) through the full lifecycle (CS → DS → CR → SH) - ⚪ DEBT → Custodian (Sentinel) for analysis, Dev for implementation - 📄 DOCS → Tech Writer (Paige)
For each assignment, create: 1. Clear task description 2. Specific files/areas to investigate 3. Acceptance criteria (how we know it's fixed) 4. Security considerations 5. Dependencies on other issues

step.goal: Update agent mission briefings and communication channels
step.action: For each assigned agent: 1. Update or create team/_memory/{agent-name}/mission.md with new assignments 2. If the agent already has a mission, APPEND new issues — don't overwrite existing assignments 3. Mark priority clearly so the agent knows what to tackle first
Mark all processed communications as processed: - Update frontmatter in team/_memory/_comms/findings/ files - Update frontmatter in team/_memory/_comms/requests/ files
If any CEO escalation items exist, prepare proposals using the CEO Approval Gate: - Reference team/engine/ceo-approval.xml - Create proposals in team/_memory/_comms/proposals/

step.goal: Present the triage summary to the CEO
step.action:
Display the triage report:
"⚔️ ISSUE TRIAGE REPORT | {date} ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
ISSUES FOUND: [total count] 🔴 Security: [count] 🟠 Bugs: [count] 🟡 Design: [count] 🔵 Build: [count] ⚪ Debt: [count] 📄 Docs: [count]
CRITICAL/HIGH ISSUES: | # | Type | Issue | Agent | Action | CEO? | |---|------|-------|-------|--------|------| [table of critical and high priority issues]
MEDIUM/LOW ISSUES: [summary count, not full table — CEO doesn't need to review these]
REQUIRING YOUR APPROVAL: [list of items that need CEO sign-off, with proposal references]
AGENTS DEPLOYED: [which agents got updated mission briefings]
═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Next: Address critical issues first. Say NA for next action."
